purpose: Project detection, context loading, and CLI binary discovery. loadProject inspects a directory for language-defining files (package.json, tsconfig.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, Gemfile, pom.xml, build.gradle), prefers the package.json `name` for display, and locates CLAUDE.md at the root + one level deep. getProjectContext reads every CLAUDE.md and returns {path, content} pairs for prompt injection. detectClaudeCLI / detectCodexCLI use `which` + a list of common install paths to find the CLI binary so swarms work without manual configuration.
gotchas: CLAUDE.md scan only goes ONE directory level deep — files at deeper nesting (subapp/feature/CLAUDE.md) are missed. This is intentional for performance but can surprise users with monorepos.
DETECT_FILES is checked in array order and FIRST MATCH wins — a project with both package.json AND requirements.txt resolves to 'javascript', not 'python'.
The name precedence is: package.json `name` field > directory basename. A project with a different package.json name than its folder will display under the package name.
detectCliBinary uses execSync('which ...', {timeout:5000}) — if PATH is non-standard (Electron may have inherited a minimal env on macOS Launchpad start) the binary is not found even if it exists.

detectCodexCLI checks /opt/homebrew/bin/codex (Apple Silicon Homebrew) BEFORE /usr/local/bin/codex (Intel Homebrew); detectClaudeCLI checks /usr/local/bin first instead — the orderings differ for the two providers and reflect typical install locations.

The fn name `getProjectContextWithFallback` retains 'Fallback' for backward compat but the behavior is ADDITIVE (concatenates primary + each additional dir's contexts). Reading the name as 'use additional only if primary empty' would be wrong.
patterns: Language detection via signature-file lookup table (DETECT_FILES).
scanDirectory builds a tree-shaped node array with directories first then alphabetical within each level — used for the file-tree display in the project setup modal.
Multi-source project context: primary CLAUDE.md + additional context dirs are merged into a single contexts[] array, later partitioned in SwarmOrchestrator.dispatchReady by path-prefix match.
conventions: Sync fs APIs throughout — invoked at IPC time, never in hot loops.
Skip dotfile dirs and node_modules/dist when scanning.
Defensive try/catch around every fs.readFileSync — partial results are preferred over hard failures.
